header_summary:
Recent discussions in blockchain technology highlight strategic and security considerations in the face of quantum computing advancements. The potential for quantum entities to exploit Bitcoin's blockchain by solving public puzzles or colluding with miners to extract $215 billion worth of legacy coins underscores the urgency for quantum-resistant strategies and the reevaluation of blockchain security frameworks. This analysis is detailed at [Bitcoin Developer Mailing List](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/CALZpt+FOUJF3E7YDk5xh-Cv9kxduGiuOPVK5x171=25C3ryJPQ@mail.gmail.com/T/#u#m2143c378036549c5d3d655b1e62399a738ccf14a).

The release of Bitcoin Core version 31.1 introduces significant improvements including bug fixes, performance enhancements, and privacy measures, with critical fixes in testing frameworks and continuous integration processes. Users are advised to follow specific upgrade procedures detailed on the [official website](https://bitcoincore.org/bin/bitcoin-core-31.1/) and report issues on [GitHub](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues).

Innovations in transaction handling within Bitcoin's network involve new serialization processes and witness styles to ensure backward compatibility and security across varying node capabilities. This development addresses concerns over transaction types and peer-to-peer layer interactions, with detailed discussions available at [Delving Bitcoin](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/segwit-commitment-to-post-quantum-witness-data/2702/3).

Furthermore, a new protocol for trustless onchain coin-flips has been introduced, featuring enhanced privacy and minimal onchain footprint, using simple Zero-Knowledge Proofs to facilitate discreet value transfers and fair bets between participants. Technical documentation and practical applications of this protocol can be explored at [Babilonia GitHub](https://github.com/AdamISZ/babilonia) and through example transactions on [Signet test network](https://mempool.space/signet/tx/59bb7633314b35d2edcff7997281c0254e9a9b36faf22dbc643d4a319d92f064).

recent_posts.title: The game-theory problems of PQ sunsetting modes
recent_posts.summary:
- Game-theory problems in post-quantum Bitcoin scenarios involve strategic security and economic impacts.
- Scenarios include quantum entity collusion with miners and potential extraction of $215 billion worth legacy coins.
- Further research is vital to address quantum computing challenges to Bitcoin's network security and stability.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core v31.1 Released
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core version 31.1 features important updates and is available for download.
- Users should fully shut down older versions before installing the new update.
- The release includes significant technical enhancements for better performance and security.

recent_posts.title: Segwit commitment to post-quantum witness data?
recent_posts.summary:
- Each transaction is assigned a distinct style number reflecting its witness type.
- A flag byte update in serialization enhances transaction type differentiation.
- Unsupported witness styles can be converted to a basic style for compatibility.

recent_posts.title: zkPoH: Zero-Knowledge Proof-of-Hodl
recent_posts.summary:
- Privacy-preserving proofs in blockchain are advancing, focusing on Bitcoin's taproot.
- Techniques like Bulletproofs use elliptic curve zero-knowledge proof to enhance efficiency.
- Generalized ZKP methods are expanding to networks like Lightning for broader applications.

recent_posts.title: Babilonia - probabilistic coinjoin and covert betting
recent_posts.summary:
- A new protocol enhances cryptocurrency transactions with improved privacy and efficiency.
- The protocol includes unique Zero-Knowledge Proof techniques and covert value transfers.
- Technical documentation and practical implementations are publicly accessible for review.

active_posts.title: DahLIAS: Discrete Logarithm-Based Interactive Aggregate Signatures
active_posts.summary:
- DahLIAS introduces a constant-size signature scheme essential for Bitcoin transactions.
- It ensures operational efficiency with a two-round signing process and rapid verifications.
- The authors encourage feedback to enhance cryptographic solutions in the Bitcoin ecosystem.

active_posts.title: BIP draft: Full-Aggregation of BIP 340 Signatures
active_posts.summary: - A new Bitcoin Improvement Proposal focuses on efficient signature aggregation.  - The proposal facilitates single signatures for multiple transactions using a two-round protocol.  - Stakeholders are invited to review and comment on the proposal via GitHub.

active_posts.title: [Research] Bitcoin After Block Rewards: preventing miner deviation as the block rewards approaches zero
active_posts.summary:
- The paper analyzes security risks for Bitcoin when block rewards end, favoring fee-based incentives.
- It identifies a threshold where miners' deviation becomes economically rational without block rewards.
- Proposed strategies include base fees and adaptive block sizing to maintain network integrity.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
active_posts.summary:
- SegData introduces a new method for data storage within Bitcoin blocks via a soft-fork.
- It relies on optional node-based data retention post-validation, and features prunable data entries.
- Implementation questions remain about witness versions, transaction weight, and privacy protections.

active_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal improves BIP-360 by optimizing EC signature recovery in P2MR structures.
- It reduces witness size by deriving public keys directly from signatures, avoiding scripts.
- Enhanced security is ensured through unique nonce derivation and signature validation adjustments.

active_posts.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
active_posts.summary: - Bitcoin privacy enhancements focus on sidechains, L2s, and wallet technologies.  - Cost-reducing technologies like CISA don't inherently increase privacy but optimize existing measures.  - The shift in Bitcoin prioritizes regulatory compatibility and minimal protocol changes.

today_in_history_posts.title: Thoughts on soft-fork activation
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin developers debate methods for future soft fork activations.
- BIP 8 and a "decreasing threshold" approach stand out, each offering different timelines.
- Proposed changes involve updating and maintaining both strategies and supporting miners via BIP 91.

today_in_history_posts.title: Dynamic Commitments: Upgrading Channels Without On-Chain Transactions
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Rusty introduces dual-opt-in with `commit_switch` and `commit_switch_reply` messages.
- He advocates for a linear upgrade of channel types, favoring newer versions.
- Upgrades involve feature bit for anchor_outputs and handling of non-compatible features.

today_in_history_posts.title: Changes to BIP-360 - Pay to Quantum Resistant Hash (P2QRH)
today_in_history_posts.summary: - BIP-360 modifications improve Bitcoin's protection against quantum attacks by updating scripts.  - The proposal separates PQ signature specs from BIP-360, enabling independent development.  - An upcoming BIP will detail PQ signatures, enhancing Bitcoin’s quantum resistance further.
